name: Troy Downing
subject: China + Russia vs. You & Me.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
Xi & Putin sent a warning. Here’s my response.

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDkxMF9JVi1EWC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3MDUyMjEtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTA5MTBfSVYtRFguMTEwMTE3X3QxNzA1MjIxLTI4OTkifQ/Ylu5YyvCu89xbJG2KsyZ0w)Peter,
Communist China is cozying up to Russia while the world watches, and America can’t afford to stand by.
For years, Communist China has been going after America’s most critical resources. They’ve stolen private data, grabbed our technology, and spent billions buying up American land.
**Now, they’re strengthening ties with Russia, a country that openly challenges global stability.**
_This is a dire warning to America, and we must act now before it’s too late. _
We need bold, unwavering leadership to defend our country and secure America’s future. As a proud America-First Patriot, I’m committed to protecting our freedoms from foreign threats and ensuring America stays strong, resilient, and free.
[Peter, contribute today to a campaign determined to stop the CCP from threatening our economy, stealing our jobs, and challenging America’s security.](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDkxMF9JVi1EWC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3MDUyMjEtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTA5MTBfSVYtRFguMTEwMTE3X3QxNzA1MjIxLTI4OTkifQ/KHleLmNBHLaZkqUGDVBg_A)[SUPPORT TROY’S AMERICA FIRST FIGHT
](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDkxMF9JVi1EWC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3MDUyMjEtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTA5MTBfSVYtRFguMTEwMTE3X3QxNzA1MjIxLTI4OTkifQ/QD0n3q0j-_vsya3-2pBhGQ)Your support never goes overlooked. Thank you for your hand in ensuring our nation stays resilient and independent.

Best,[](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MDkxMF9JVi1EWC4xMTAxMTdfdDE3MDUyMjEtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTA5MTBfSVYtRFguMTEwMTE3X3QxNzA1MjIxLTI4OTkifQ/J9z3ZqP4qdNe_aVfE8jNFw)Troy Downing _Patriot in Congress_| |

name: Wes Moore
subject: These early days matter. All eyes are on us right now.
body:
The strength of our grassroots support in the first 48 hours will set the tone for the entire race.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)
Good morning, Peter.
Every morning before the sun comes up, I ground myself in service. I say a prayer of thanks and head to work out. **But yesterday felt different.**
It was the first day of our campaign for reelection – the start of a new chapter. Not just for me, Peter, but for our shared mission to build a state that leaves no one behind.
Thanks to the hundreds of supporters on this team who’ve already chipped in, our reelection campaign is off to an incredible start. But here’s the thing, Peter: I can’t win this race without you.
**[Can I count on you to be one of the very first supporters in PA to step up to become a Founding Donor? If you’re ready to join me as I run for reelection as governor of Maryland, please chip in today.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250910-1?refcode=em-20250910-1&amount=5&refcode2=590_1086043_yJxkhI&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)**
**[Donate $5 to become a Founding Donor »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250910-1?amount=5&refcode=em-20250910-1&refcode2=590_1086043_yJxkhI&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)**
When I sit down with my team tonight to review our list of Founding Donors, I really want to see your name on that list.
Together, we will protect the progress we’ve made and keep delivering results for every day for Maryland.
**[Would you please become a Founding Donor of our campaign before the end of today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250910-1?refcode=em-20250910-1&amount=5&refcode2=590_1086043_yJxkhI&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)**
The strength of our grassroots support in the first **48 hours** will set the tone for the entire race.
Thank you for being on our team.
Elevate,
Wes
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-20250910-1?refcode=em-20250910-1&refcode2=590_1086043_yJxkhI&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)

* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wes-em-footer?refcode=em-20250910-1&t=2&refcode2=590_1086043_yJxkhI&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.wesmoore.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.wesmoore.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=590%2E1086043%2EyJxkhI)

Email us: [info@wesmoore.com](mailto:info@wesmoore.com)
BY AUTHORITY: WES MOORE FOR MARYLAND, MARY TYDINGS, TREASURER

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1813, during the War of 1812, American Naval forces under the command of Oliver H. Perry [successfully attacked the British fleet](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u6zAQhE9DdRKWP5bEgsVr1Pm1KYWlubSZrESDZCz49oETIBdIN8AM5gO-t_N6xsRpv_7HjVyLed3pqEytUVlr-wy0t3rJhWpEZo-VPDKvCtSpB9tLWAMmfq6mCy4SKdIdOTmdJmPnUUJ3c6C00agsRTn6ANrO86QQ7eg1TjipLrnXG1gJEkBrGOIcEYzXl3E01pAXBiiklsvwntPebhT5mR9UhkveOna31u5V6H9CLUItx3EMHltjiok41CGXq1ALE5b9VWPp5SyVUMvPqv6mnvGDeiqJuuLC_UjMqY6TMHDdMPE3rf1J0cOprwAAAP__WLl6fQ) in Lake Erie and claimed supremacy over the lake. Education isn't what it once was. The latest Nation’s Report Card shows [test scores for U.S.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unTAQhJ8GOqPFmL_CRRq6mzYlWuw1OFrsI9scdN4-IlKUB7jdJ400mm9-fa1f6NmH_SeepIuLa6A7M5VCac3lshRKNjFRdsi8YaYNmVcJshcwixZWi54_q6qtdkSSupp0O_ajmqehhfrQFt3Wu20aBxzlrMw0gZ1odJPtZUvY1V4_bTC30AJ0HTRucghq68wwqFnRVikg60tMze_oQznI8Se-KTUmnjXro5RXrroflVwqudz33YTNPBZPXsnlwUouVxb_6fD7IbI5YmTxz1JwzCT2FK9gxYnlEInQ-rALE0Px4XrwQ5gyx7CLZALKDqCFOmn7uj2zz8NYKdhP9Px3XPnWo28t_wQAAP__dluNzA) high school seniors have fallen to their lowest levels in decades, with sharp declines in math, reading, and science. Read more below. Today's sponsor, [Pacaso](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OnTAQhZ_GdKDxDwYKF5FWt9u0Ka0Bjy-OjI1sc1f79hGbKC-w7TmfZo6-X-_2HUMM6fkTDzLNZ5voo0ZqjYqt7XKUWt1yoeoxxhUrrRijFSDGHpaeg3UY4qdVnTOeSJDsyPBpnNQyaw7dbnBSyEcNenXz6MU0T94v3q1EeuRugy6Y-xosHDiAlDD42SOoVW5aq0XRyhSQCy2X4XcOqe3k42d-URm2fHTR7K2dlckfTDyYeIT0otqGEzes-SbuWD6udtiar7IRk290YIhM6Ds8yIXrYPLtxOD6E0tLVOoezn_9hseJ4Zm-iP8ln6GXo7R_FVwpNLQcpnGWEmZYumLc-RFiDFVPTMHzfvg1t33L8cuIPwEAAP__-NyQzA), a fast-growing vacation home co-ownership company backed by top investors, offers everyday investors the chance to join at just [$2.90/share](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OnTAQhZ_GdKDxDwYKF5FWt9u0Ka0Bjy-OjI1sc1f79hGbKC-w7TmfZo6-X-_2HUMM6fkTDzLNZ5voo0ZqjYqt7XKUWt1yoeoxxhUrrRijFSDGHpaeg3UY4qdVnTOeSJDsyPBpnNQyaw7dbnBSyEcNenXz6MU0T94v3q1EeuRugy6Y-xosHDiAlDD42SOoVW5aq0XRyhSQCy2X4XcOqe3k42d-URm2fHTR7K2dlckfTDyYeIT0otqGEzes-SbuWD6udtiar7IRk290YIhM6Ds8yIXrYPLtxOD6E0tLVOoezn_9hseJ4Zm-iP8ln6GXo7R_FVwpNLQcpnGWEmZYumLc-RFiDFVPTMHzfvg1t33L8cuIPwEAAP__-NyQzA).

## Math, Reading Scores Hit 20-Year Low
High school seniors’ reading and math scores have fallen to [their lowest levels in more than 20 years](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2unTAQhJ8GOqPFmL_CRRq6mzYlWuw1OFrsI9scdN4-IlKUB7jdJ400mm9-fa1f6NmH_SeepIuLa6A7M5VCac3lshRKNjFRdsi8YaYNmVcJshcwixZWi54_q6qtdkSSupp0O_ajmqehhfrQFt3Wu20aBxzlrMw0gZ1odJPtZUvY1V4_bTC30AJ0HTRucghq68wwqFnRVikg60tMze_oQznI8Se-KTUmnjXro5RXrroflVwqudz33YTNPBZPXsnlwUouVxb_6fD7IbI5YmTxz1JwzCT2FK9gxYnlEInQ-rALE0Px4XrwQ5gyx7CLZALKDqCFOmn7uj2zz8NYKdhP9Px3XPnWo28t_wQAAP__dluNzA), according to the latest National Assessment of Education Progress. The report found 45 percent of 12th graders scored below basic in math and 32 percent below basic in reading, while eighth-grade science scores also dropped. Education Secretary Linda McMahon blamed federal spending programs and said the Trump administration [will push to return control to states.](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOgzAQRU9jOkeDbQgULrahy7ZbImMP2KsBR_YkUW6_IlfYdp7mf73_c5tvLlE6tm-3o-U1zwe-KiEzlrnyI-DB1eeCdXVEi6u4OKJZgeokjLKFObhE79k0wa6ICnWDtr12VzMOfQtNtAh-WVvlh2HEQXd-dKbTfY-dQaW86ZtkzzQYW2gBtIbLOqwOzKJ935vR4CIMYEicy-U3p4MjrvTOTywXn_eGbGS-V6G_hJqEmjhiTEQnE2qKecdTSKgJw8M7TvkQaurMqA10MqYtyupjzoSlyoIupGOTu-PzfFrL4_NzsnsuLL0rQaipKTbcX4ko1f4qDGy7S5_Ohv814tOqvwAAAP__-OWHaw) Others blame screens, smartphones, poor teaching methods and the COVID-19 pandemic for the continuing decline. Data shows that chronic absenteeism and pandemic-era disruptions worsened the situation, but the overall trend began before COVID hit in 2020.
## Israel Attacks Hamas Leaders in Qatar
Israel launched an [airstrike on Hamas leaders in Doha, Qatar](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O5CAQhJ8GZ4wwMP4JCC5xNpdeaLWhGXPXNj7oHWvefuWV9gk2qaSkT1Xfn8f8gERpf_6GDR3HPO94VkJmLHPlj4A7V58L1ghEC1RcgGjWSt-lGmWr5gCJ3rNtgouIGk2Dru3vvR2HrlXN6sKAZjQYfBdxia3vfcQBWu-NMnbErknuoqmxVa1SxqhbHCIouxjfdXa0uAirMCTO5fY3p51XjPTOLyw3n7eG3Mp8VGF-CT0JPe3vI1e-KqGnCyz0pMbvOHOhIK-LQk-pFkCSDOWJXOUKG1RJCAFLlWfiVVYu6R_KvMv_wFCq9HAkBhJ6aooLx5mIUu16YdVzg0Rfi_hHGl9OfwYAAP__SyGH5g), on Tuesday, striking their political headquarters as they discussed a U.S.-backed Gaza cease-fire plan. Hamas claimed its senior officials survived, though five members of its team were killed along with the son of top negotiator Khalil al-Hayya. Qatar said one of its security officers also died. The White House confirmed it [had advance notice](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u4zAQRk9DdTLIEa2fgsU26rztlsJQHFqzoUSHHNtwTh84V0j9Ad_De_8uywU58XH9izs5iXk56FkTiVBZqtwDHVLXXKhGTMljJY8pLaDh3OqpNXoJyOm12Ca4SATUNeTMcB7sNPZGN5uDgFZ7jxH86EPsjTFDHMBMhN0Uo27Yvd_0ZLTRuuv0KY4RtfXd2vd2suSV1RRYcjn9z3zIRjG98oPKac17k9wmcquq-6NgVjDLRhun9N4UzFve6S2kYMaw88FVCgrnQ8F8tpOFEVop9_3WroWFV_6i2nItSKmtUviD2k8ULArmprhwe3JKXPtBWX3dkX8wjfyq28PBdwAAAP__FhuDnw) and relayed a last-minute warning to Qatar, later calling the attack an “unfortunate incident” that undermines peace efforts. The strike drew swift condemnation from Qatar, the UN, and U.S. allies from Britain to Germany, while Arab states denounced it as a violation of sovereignty. Here's [CCTV footage of the bombing](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1u6zAQBODTUJ2F5S4tWQWL16jza1MK_FnaDNaiIzIWfPtAuUK6AQb4gJmP63J1WfJ6--8ebFsqy8p7FW6Nt6W278hrq6FsXJMT8a6ydyILAp5PMJ00LNFleS-mizYxI1PHVo_n0UyXQUN3t-Bj5DCwRprwQqTPRCNwCI50IkxdtocGkwYNQAR9uiQHxlMYBjMZ9soAx9zK1n-WvLY7J3mXF299KI9O7L21Z1X0T-GscN73vfc-HJ3C-RijcH7lyOUIQccvmhDMvpVus_G5Z5Fch1EZuD1cll-z_emIl8WfAAAA__9toXEM).
## Nepal Youth Burn Parliament Over Social Media Ban
Mass protests in Nepal erupted after the [government blocked Facebook](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAUhJ8GOiP_rTGFi9vQ7W1TIhsfL44OeGWfLNo8fUReId1II83o-z7uy91nzMfjv9_BUSrLAWdDIIK6NPqKcFBbS4WWPGLwDYJHXCSXN8YnJvgSfcb3ovvoEoAE1YMT423UkzWC95tb0zSaYG00ioOVKhoRo1LCWB2C9rc-u2uNT4ILzpXiQ7LJcx3UaoyeNIROc4iZSh0-Sz5og4Tv8oI6rGXv0W1Ez9apf52cOzmf5zm8_VbK1XZyvnA6OftKeUW44gMO9s2etRA0gtpYA2IpV2BCSiPFaMdhox376uLzzIi5mbHT_LH7jL-f9CdRLyd_AgAA__9zy3yS), YouTube, and X for failing to register under new rules. Demonstrators stormed parliament and set it on fire, along with the Prime Minister’s residence and other official buildings. [Watch a report here](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1ug0AQxfHTQGc0-8ECxRaWIneO0kROh2ZhMGuNWYsdjHz7yLlC2vekX_G_nPszRo7L9RPv5GVK_UJ7ZhKhtc-yjbRIHtJKeULmgJkCMvcadH2A7qCgHzHyq7fl6CciTaYkr5q6sV3rFJSzD_UAYahrE5RBRK1NaM3Ytk7VTqFryujfGnQKFIAxUE3thGCDGZyznaVQWKAxSlqrW4qLzDTxKz1prYZ0L9nPIo9cmGOhT4U-7ftevdImW6D3_15Qhrkwp2dhPuLxR774sn3foFz9-Ngjc8yuKSxc7xj5j5R_dXh6_RsAAP__JnRxMA). At least 19 people were killed and hundreds injured as police clashed with protesters, largely young Gen Z adults. Amid the chaos, Prime Minister KP [Sharma Oli resigned](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkM2O2yAQgJ8G34j4W4gPHFJVvqVS1UOPFpghocKAYLLWvn3lvMIeR5_0zcz3977eXcqpPH65HSzGuhY4RgZE6OvAV4CCY6sdRnQ5ezfAu5xXwcQHZTPlbA0u5a9VTcFGAAFyAsvNh1HzVXM2Pa32PvAQOFcmGO6V81rPVzPPQhojNUzJnjY2c8YZk5Jd4jU6przctFazAk8Ug5Cw9su_mgo-Ieav-gn9stV9yvaJ2AaRNyIWIpbjOC77KCcjYoFCX4OI5XzqhLXn8B6by7S5npPboSAdgLQWGlMH6iJCp22nHUZ6lEHPXdQVTHSrvb8aplpo6xVh4Gl1nd5u_B7470DkUrcUiPz542wKx_gDvU3dhnaknNPQhij22F3K7-vxW8k_rfgfAAD__9obl_A). Protesters say this revolt is not only about wanting their social media back, and that it's also a demand to end corruption, authoritarianism, and an unresponsive government.

clean_body: High school seniors’ reading and math scores have fallen to , according to the latest National Assessment of Education Progress. | | | | | | Wednesday, September 10, 2025 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1813, during the War of 1812, American Naval forces under the command of Oliver H. Perry in Lake Erie and claimed supremacy over the lake. Education isn't what it once was. The latest Nation’s Report Card shows high school seniors have fallen to their lowest levels in decades, with sharp declines in math, reading, and science. Read more below. Today's sponsor, , a fast-growing vacation home co-ownership company backed by top investors, offers everyday investors the chance to join at just . | Math, Reading Scores Hit 20-Year Low High school seniors’ reading and math scores have fallen to , according to the latest National Assessment of Education Progress. The report found 45 percent of 12th graders scored below basic in math and 32 percent below basic in reading, while eighth-grade science scores also dropped. Education Secretary Linda McMahon blamed federal spending programs and said the Trump administration Others blame screens, smartphones, poor teaching methods and the COVID-19 pandemic for the continuing decline. Data shows that chronic absenteeism and pandemic-era disruptions worsened the situation, but the overall trend began before COVID hit in 2020. Israel Attacks Hamas Leaders in Qatar Israel launched an , on Tuesday, striking their political headquarters as they discussed a U.S.-backed Gaza cease-fire plan. Hamas claimed its senior officials survived, though five members of its team were killed along with the son of top negotiator Khalil al-Hayya. Qatar said one of its security officers also died. The White House confirmed it and relayed a last-minute warning to Qatar, later calling the attack an “unfortunate incident” that undermines peace efforts. The strike drew swift condemnation from Qatar, the UN, and U.S. allies from Britain to Germany, while Arab states denounced it as a violation of sovereignty. Here's . Nepal Youth Burn Parliament Over Social Media Ban Mass protests in Nepal erupted after the , YouTube, and X for failing to register under new rules. Demonstrators stormed parliament and set it on fire, along with the Prime Minister’s residence and other official buildings. . At least 19 people were killed and hundreds injured as police clashed with protesters, largely young Gen Z adults. Amid the chaos, Prime Minister KP . Protesters say this revolt is not only about wanting their social media back, and that it's also a demand to end corruption, authoritarianism, and an unresponsive government. ## Talarico Announces Senate Run
Democratic State Rep. James Talarico of Round Rock—an aspiring minister and former teacher who was elected to the Texas House in 2018—announced his [candidacy for the](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd7aG8R8UFLdxl9tuaWEYEiQMkT0bJ2-_clZ5gm2mGp3vfOfrMl9sTDFf_9uVDD9DmTMdeyJm2ma2yW7RFcpM275TtkybdcQzAvY16FrC7G1Mr7mrvFnAa1mRkWM_9oCyU9XNeKctOZCAuvVAelFK94t3QwsddpKqaM4w0BKkBMShWZZBdSG0QfUQNCrRAfnIZWv4RkxPu4f0Kg_aGlfWKpkb830X7T-Bk8DpOI7mXlLk6Mr5IHA6jQROJ0bgBPpzPnp1CSG6aFNtcy7f2dFKmes3qv61rgH6HpQaq834-xFTivswig6uq43pXYT_Nt_D4E8AAAD__xOsg0Y)[ U.S. Senate](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAQhZ8Gd7aG8R8UFLdxl9tuaWEYEiQMkT0bJ2-_clZ5gm2mGp3vfOfrMl9sTDFf_9uVDD9DmTMdeyJm2ma2yW7RFcpM275TtkybdcQzAvY16FrC7G1Mr7mrvFnAa1mRkWM_9oCyU9XNeKctOZCAuvVAelFK94t3QwsddpKqaM4w0BKkBMShWZZBdSG0QfUQNCrRAfnIZWv4RkxPu4f0Kg_aGlfWKpkb830X7T-Bk8DpOI7mXlLk6Mr5IHA6jQROJ0bgBPpzPnp1CSG6aFNtcy7f2dFKmes3qv61rgH6HpQaq834-xFTivswig6uq43pXYT_Nt_D4E8AAAD__xOsg0Y) yesterday, promising to "bring people together and take power back for working Texans." Talarico's [profile has risen](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3SAQRb8Gd1gDxjYuKNK427QprXkw7CPCxoLJet_fR94oX7Dt1dXRPffX2_aGKafj_Sfu5Pgzlu2gq2ViproxZqzJFzqYamt0IFNFT7xp0KOERSrYAqb82kwX3APCojpyah7nEbQytns6A2BmA4OeYvC40BAma23AeQmDid50yd0wWBQoBVpP_eMxWRPjEO0IcdFWGKCQuNSen8T0iS3mV_mg2vuyd9k9mc8mhh9Cr0Kv13X1-6vhgQeXI5W7JPR6Wwm95uIxC72eJSdO_o6wcvKZhF6_2LLSKX_jTk3-15d_mvwnL7WCwY4w9efz7KoL55VyTm2ahYH3HVP-2sTfe_LD6b8BAAD__xZ9iTA) in the past year after an appearance on Joe Rogan's podcast. He has vocally opposed legislation that established a new school choice program and required posting the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms. Talarico, 36, joins former U.S. Rep. Colin Allred and retired NASA astronaut Terry Virts in [seeking the Democratic nomination](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb_G3mw9XjC2B4Yu2dK1o4XhEdNiHMFTSP6-SqR-Qferc3XO12W5mBBDun6anTQ__LEkqiUSM-WFTTQ52IMSUy6FkmHKxhIvCDh0MHcCFmdCfC6ydXoFN4uWtBiHcQAUcmo3rUCisDgNUoEjMSo1zXaQpNy6ejXaNugXDGYBQgCi6tdVTdL7k58G8DNOjQRygY_c80ZMD1N8fB53yr099jbqjflWmtNHg-cGz7XW_qVQiX5egwbP32an0v3JdHYzyVLpakgppGv3RnYocASc26zdrYYYQ1FjI-G6mxDfR_y_PHeNvwEAAP__MQl6DQ). Four-term incumbent John Cornyn and Attorney General Ken Paxton are in a tight race for the Republican nod. "_This is an underdog fight. We're going up against the political establishment, and we're going up against a lot of money. Big Money is powerful, but it's nothing compared to people power_ ,” [Talarico said](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1y6yAQBeCnQR0aWOsHFRS3UefbptSs0GKTIOGBjWW_fUZx8gRpd2fOme-8naczhhi2y39cyfLDp2mjvURipjwxRszBJdqYcim0IVNGRzyBglaqQWo1LRjic2qqxc5qGXRFVvdt3yrQjamu1invEND00OLcOK31PHdDY2hYlPGzqYI9wtSgldYKoKvnuTON9ydvWuUHMKJRtAROueYrMT2w-PhMd8q1S2sV7ZX5VsTpn4BRwLjve_3xwO14ChgPjYDxlmLg4DDKn8M7rlTkL1C-hPLASZ-y_JRFvrwCxirb5baHGEPpetGoy4ohfpfz3ya7W_gKAAD__9B1gh8).
## Texas A&M Faculty Removed Over Viral Video
A Texas A&M dean and department head [have been removed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQBuCnsTesg-BgBoYu3tK1o4XNkaBisOAcJ29fuVKfoMstJ33_r__rNt1siCHdP-2Khl4-TwmPGpEIy0Q22hKWjImw1IrJEha7IE0CRM9AMw6TsyG-J9k6M4PTvEXDVa96EFwO7cPgRTuOs9NKKuVBy17CLJToFfeD5boN5sRAc-AchLh283wdpPcXP_TgtRgaCegC5dLRAwlftvr4zk8s3ZLXNpoH0Vaby0cjxkaMx3F03zO9zmcjxtNuxAj679iVObSJbSV7rDUXVnDNT3TM5xjzEdKdVdodJmJLXrdoQ6LKzjy27KWEZY_7KbfFuO0IMYZ6VY2E-2pD_K1E_xvyacRPAAAA__93qIhj) from their positions after State Rep. Brian Harrison (R-Midlothian) shared videos of a confrontation between a student and a professor of children's literature this summer regarding instruction related to gender identity. In the video, the unnamed female student asks Dr. Melissa McCoul if it's legal to teach about gender identity in light of President Donald Trump's [executive order](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbvOnDAQhZ_GdEb2AAsUFGno_rQpkbHHMJIvK3t22X37iLxC6qNz0_fnZ_sxFCgdv03EhT8-bwmvGpAZy8YmmEI2Y2IstWIyjMVY5A0UDFLNUqvNGQrfrW_csis36wYXPQ7joED3U3MuAGBHa2GwONl9hEF3c-dnP3s_m85CQ8sdpmattFYAj3bfH1PvfeenQfkZJtErdMS5tHwi48dUH775jaW1OTZhOZmfVXS_BKwC1uu62uskxjO_KrZHfgtYnwUrOUxMJkhjmXKqAta7WMCqtIDVocfkKB3yyhGT9CVHeWByWCQ5zCEfX4kfLhipRmmSkwUr53Jbdrp1siZILi8-JWfJJ0qPDosJ8rjnpoiJBaxNWdzzohCoPkbRqyMaCv--8P8ReC_wNwAA___bMp-5) denying federal funds to institutions that "promote gender ideology." The professor insists her teaching is not illegal and asks the student to leave. Harrison also [shared](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2O6yAQQOGnwV2iYQwYCorbuMttt7QGexwjETuC2fy8_Sr7Ctue4ivO12W6UC55v_6nG0d5rce087MVFuE6CRWqeT54F66t8U7ClWaWCQHtCcJJw7RQLu_JdEtMsATdcdSDHSygNr7bopktDr1JfeIZrQuztUsYDHufTOoddTl-MAgatAZEd07JebOu_eotrAG9MsBLlqOeZWPhF7W1vI8H1_N83LoSN5F7U_0_haPC8fWpCsdUM-28Ua25HbvCsQnJd1M46uAshN4bbxEwGOtsV-Nyf-ZScnODMnC9US6_vPxtyiPiTwAAAP__Z7dzvw) audio recordings of a conversation the student had with President Welsh, who insisted he would not fire McCoul but acknowledged course descriptions should accurately reflect the subject matter. Other social media posts provided [copies of course materials](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL0O4jAQhJ_G6YzsTchP4YImHddeGW3wGizZMVpvCLz9CV7hupFGGs33_b0uV4wpbvc_mMnJO5Rlo6MmEiFeBBNyvBXahLhW2lCI8UaygIGzNpO2ZvEY02fpGu9W4yfbkLPDeTgbsN3YPNwwdt5gT9YAtjQO_Rq61oDpwgq2naCJ7jtmJmusNQD9aV37sQuhDePZhAlG1RnyUQqf5EFCb6whfcqL-HQruUnuIfKsqr0omBXMx3Gcct2-nYKZNr1XBfMXSsH8y6BRZx3wtif56Ex5Ja6aKZcXeR24ZM0lUdUYhFjfafPEusruI1W9P7kgK5iR9eVir_75poadfx4xpVj7QXXmnjGm3zv5P6cvB_8CAAD__7fRjH0), including an infographic called the "Gender Unicorn." In a statement, Texas A&M University System Chancellor Glenn Hegar said, "_It is unacceptable for A &M System faculty to push a personal political agenda_." Texas landed [four restaurants](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOpCAUxfGnwU5zuQJKQbGN3bftlgb1MpKgGLg7zrz9ZuYVtj7J7yT_Pz_zj48pno_f_iDHr5Dnk-6aiJnKzD75EtdMJ1OplU7PVPxKPCOgbsG2EubNx_SeVbO5BTYrG3Jy0IMGlGpsdrdabTz2RoFWg9XoV9gWSwsZMxAF00T3wcBKkBIQTbcsZlQh9GHUECyOQgFtkXPpeCeml68hvfOTSrfmo0luZ76q6H8JnARO9313617y-VkFTiHnTeDkC8c1kcDpK7SFKvu_xZ9cW44H1Zbz1WpoUUJvAVV37VdT3HbdMaVYzSAUPA4f0_eV_6_V0-G_AAAA__9dzX3z) on the annual _New York Times_ list of the country's 50 best restaurants, which was released Monday. subject: 26 Maine Students Named National Merit Semifinalists
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** 26 Maine Students Named National Merit Semifinalists (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=3d6ed71d9a&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Steve Robinson on Sep 10, 2025 06:32 am
Twenty-six Maine high school seniors have been named semifinalists in the 2026 National Merit Scholarship Program, the National Merit Scholarship Corporation announced Wednesday. The students are among more than 16,000 semifinalists nationwide who will compete for nearly $26 million in scholarships next spring. To advance to finalist status, students must submit a detailed application, maintain [...]

------------------------------------------------------------

Camden cops are hoping someone drops a dime on the pink-stained political activist who damaged $175,000 worth of spanking-new parking kiosks. name: Elise Stefanik
subject: President Trump called me a Great Warrior
body:
I need 1,000 great warriors to stand with me right now.

| | | | |
---
Friend, I received a message I’ll never forget.
**[President Trump called me a "GREAT WARRIOR!"](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
From day one **I fought hard against the outrageous _$464 million judgement_ again President Trump in New York** and when it finally got tossed out, I was overjoyed.
The Left weaponized the justice system in a desperate attempt to take down President Trump.
**I refused to stay silent.** I filed formal ethics complaints, exposed misconduct, and stood up for the rule of law when it mattered most.
This was about justice.**_And justice prevailed._**
My efforts to defend President Trump haven't gone unnoticed. This means a lot to me but what means even more is what it represents.
**We stood our ground. We fought back.****_And we won!_**
Now, I want to ask you to stand with us. I’m building a national list of **1,000 Great American Warriors** who I know are ready to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with me and President Trump as we continue to fight for this great country.
_**[Friend, I want to see you on that list of patriots](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_**!**

[ADD YOUR NAME - FIGHT, FIGHT, FIGHT](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
We need more warriors and I am counting on you.
I hope that you choose to add your name and then make a contribution to my team's efforts to defend President Trump and Save America.

_With gratitude,_
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**Elise Stefanik**
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/b9pa/?__vbtrk=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MzE4MzA0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3d7c333a-023b-4b59-9fa4-67f0cdfce61f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect: after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t. When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families. Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can._ | Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC | | or

# ![Talarico for Texas][1]

Hi Peter,

Thank you for signing up to join Team Talarico!

We’re building a people-powered campaign to win this Senate race, and we’re so
excited you’re a part of it.

**📱 Follow us on social media**
Stay in touch with us on [Facebook][2], [X][3], [Instagram][4], [TikTok][5],
and [YouTube.][6] Your comments, likes, and reposts keep us visible and viral,
which makes a big impact!

**💲 Consider donating to our people-powered campaign**
We know you get asks like this all the time – your donations truly keep this
ship afloat. [Please click here if you’re able to contribute][7], and share
with your friends and family!

**💪 Sign up to Volunteer **
It's going to take a massive people-powered movement to win in 2026. Please
[sign up to volunteer][8] to help us go everywhere and talk to everyone!

Thank you again for joining us. We’re grateful you’re here.

\- Team Talarico

![][9]

Paid for by Talarico for Texas

Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. James is grateful to have you in
our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities
to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here][10].

name: Dominic Rapini
subject: Voter Fraud: A Cautionary Tale of Two Cities
body:
It's time for Zero Trust in Mail In Voting

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us16.campaign-archive.com/?e=8763c56857&u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=e54f91e055)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | “Absence of evidence is not evidence of absence.” - Carl Segan
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
|
---
|
---
| |
# Opinion: _Frio County, Texas, and Bridgeport, A Tale of Two Frauds_
---
| |
By Dominic Rapini, Republican Candidate for Secretary of State 2022
Voter fraud is neither hypothetical nor isolated. From the fields of Frio County, Texas, to the city streets of Bridgeport, Connecticut, **recent scandals illuminate a clear and present danger stalking our elections** : the abuse of absentee ballots and systematic ballot harvesting. These cautionary tales prove that fraud can be widespread, it does happen, and it’s dangerously difficult to expose and prosecute[11][2][3][7][5][6][1].
**Widespread and Hard to Prove**
In Frio County, Texas, six officials now face indictments for manipulating mail-in ballots and paying for harvested votes through cash or smartphone apps[11]. Their scheme targeted the elderly and disabled, using sophisticated methods to traffic ballots and conceal misconduct. Meanwhile, Bridgeport, Connecticut, witnessed one of the most significant absentee ballot fraud investigations in state history, with surveillance footage and tip-offs resulting in more than a dozen arrests and the overturning of the results of a mayoral primary [2][3][1][6]. Bridgeport operatives, city council members, party leaders, and campaign staff signed out hundreds of absentee ballot applications, illegally took possession of ballots, and sought to mislead voters about eligibility[10][5][6].
**Uncovering and Prosecuting Fraud: A Herculean Challenge**
The **Bridgeport scandal unraveled only after viral video evidence surfaced** , national news attention intensified, and a persistent investigation led to hundreds of criminal charges. [7][8][5][1] In both **Frio and Bridgeport, fraudsters exploited trust,** inadequate oversight, and the inherent vulnerabilities of absentee voting. Even with overwhelming evidence, prosecution is arduous, evidence is scattered, witnesses are fearful, and most frauds never make it to conviction[7][1][4].
**Damaging Democracy and Undermining Trust**
When fraud succeeds, it inflicts generational damage to our democracy. Voters lose faith. Turnout falls. Good candidates walk away, unwilling to risk a tainted process. Neighborhoods targeted by ballot harvesting lose their voice, and every alleged act casts doubt on the legitimacy of our elections[7][5]. The lesson from Frio and Bridgeport is crystal clear: fraud is not simply rare it is rarely caught, and its impact, especially when left unchecked, devastates the public’s trust in free and fair elections.
**The Only Answer: Zero Tolerance, Zero Trust**
If Connecticut, Texas, or any state wishes to safeguard democracy, there must be an absolute, uncompromising rejection of voter fraud. Campaigns must never be allowed to touch absentee ballot applications or ballots. Every absentee ballot application must be tied to a valid photo ID, and the law should cap campaign-initiated ballot requests at five per person solely for family or certified caregivers. Technology can help, but only vigilance, transparency, and common-sense restrictions will eliminate the raw materials of fraud[11][3][1].
**A Caution for the Nation**
Frio County’s harvesters, Bridgeport’s ballot stuffers these are warnings, not outliers. America must heed them. The legitimacy of elections cannot permit “just a little fraud.” The standard in banking, business, or government is zero. Our votes demand nothing less. **Only when we adopt zero tolerance and zero trust for fraud will we restore confidence and integrity where it matters most: the ballot box.**
**Sources**
[1] Five Charged Following Investigation into Handling of Absentee ... https://portal.ct.gov/dcj/press-releases/division-of-criminal-justice/02212025bridgeportarrests
[2] Bridgeport election probe expands; four more operatives charged https://ctmirror.org/2025/07/30/bridgeport-election-probe-expands/
[3] Bridgeport Municipal Mayoral Democratic Primary 2023 Election https://electionfraud.heritage.org/case/200379
[4] Suspect enters guilty plea in Bridgeport ballot fraud case - CT Mirror https://ctmirror.org/2025/07/23/ct-bridgeport-ballot-fraud-guilty-plea/
[5] Bridgeport Democrats in court to face election-related charges https://www.ctpublic.org/news/2025-03-06/bridgeport-absentee-ballots-election-fraud-ct-charges-court
[6] 5 charged in connection with 2023 Bridgeport election fraud - WSHU https://www.wshu.org/connecticut-news/2025-02-21/5-charged-in-connection-with-2023-bridgeport-election-fraud
[7] Election Fraud Is Rare. Except, Maybe, in Bridgeport, Conn. https://www.nytimes.com/2024/01/21/nyregion/joe-ganim-john-gomes-bridgeport-mayor-election.html
[8] 5 charged in Bridgeport absentee ballot fraud to be arraigned https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4BLQsfCIpOA
[9] Bridgeport absentee ballot fraud case yields 4 more arrests: Officials https://www.ctpost.com/news/article/bridgeport-primary-absentee-ballot-fraud-arrested-20794978.php
[10] Timeline of Bridgeport's 2023 election scandal and charges - CTPost https://www.ctpost.com/projects/2025/news/bridgeport-election-fraud-arrests-timeline/
[11] 6 people are charged in a Texas elections investigation involving ... https://www.ksat.com/news/national/2025/05/07/6-people-are-charged-in-a-texas-elections-investigation-involving-vote-harvesting/

name: This is bad
subject: Taking back the house just got harder. This makes it impossible.
body:
Democrats will need a massive blue wave but reclaiming the House is still possible. However, there is something Republicans are working on that will all but ensure Democrats never control the House
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Dpxa8qwD7HhYBIdfv3VP_G4po77zc0S63OsAJzLudZw8XDmQW1KdSKCxTlJmdzzOF5qvmQ5hgj2CVE22wegoe2x652y8HISo-PKgYU2lA5PJK5J4T-Nresrn6dX001iE2ONp_KTFoOuYadaUTPayBDuPmDpAdlqPv4nevS4nI9kZcFHkKDPJgvpy6FvVmUPTW_89IwkthIok3OO0UCO5t0OXop5-RXhFy-QbLeJ-Zbk=&c=QQhPAuux0tvvITzcB359FvHODaVMKQM6vYfKIQNCu2TQDqgLAebDHQ==&ch=jviaOwMEN6kYdPnCLTzR6LGOUcYAD-u8p5EjunEbhGucMunQvpOUkA==)

| Peter,
The upcoming 2026 midterms were shaping up to be a disaster for the GOP.
Trump's popularity is tanking, his involvement in the Epstein scandal has deeply divided the MAGA base, and House Republicans face revolts in their districts over cuts to Medicaid.
To save their House majority they pushed Texas to pass rigged maps that remove a minimum of five blue districts in one swoop. Now MAGA redistricts in Ohio, Missouri, and Florida are on the way.
The New York Times has published an infographic to show how difficult the new redistricting landscape makes it for dems to reclaim the House in 2026:
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Dpxa8qwD7HhYBIdfv3VP_G4po77zc0S63OsAJzLudZw8XDmQW1KdSKCxTlJmdzzOF5qvmQ5hgj2CVE22wegoe2x652y8HISo-PKgYU2lA5PJK5J4T-Nresrn6dX001iE2ONp_KTFoOuYadaUTPayBDuPmDpAdlqPv4nevS4nI9kZcFHkKDPJgvpy6FvVmUPTW_89IwkthIok3OO0UCO5tyNWrLTltlWYVnz-qSJUAKo=&c=QQhPAuux0tvvITzcB359FvHODaVMKQM6vYfKIQNCu2TQDqgLAebDHQ==&ch=jviaOwMEN6kYdPnCLTzR6LGOUcYAD-u8p5EjunEbhGucMunQvpOUkA==)
---
| | | | Democrats will need a massive blue wave but reclaiming the House is still possible. However, there is something Republicans are working on that will all but ensure Democrats never hold a Congressional majority again.
The crown jewel of MAGA's redistricting vision is Pennsylvania. If MAGA axed all Democratic seats here it would give them 18 red seats, enough seats to give them a near bulletproof House majority.
The only thing preventing this is the bulwark of Pennsylvania's Supreme Court which has a 3 vote democratic majority. Unfortunately, these justices are up for reelection this coming November 2025, and MAGA has launched a full court press to have them removed.
Using the lack of attention this race is getting to their advantage, MAGA has quietly prepared a warchest and on the ground presence comparable in size and scale to what a presidential campaign might deploy to flip a swing state. Democrats on the other hand have been asleep at the wheel and allowed MAGA to make massive gains over the summer which if left unchecked will cost us this race.
We have fifty five days until election day, there is still time for Dems to mount a comeback.
[Defend Our Courts is mounting the defense of the Pennsylvania Supreme Court and we are preparing for an unprecedented tidal wave of MAGA money that could soar into the hundreds of millions. Will you help us brace for impact and rush a contribution of $50, $25, or even $5?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Dpxa8qwD7HhYBIdfv3VP_G4po77zc0S63OsAJzLudZw8XDmQW1KdSKCxTlJmdzzOF5qvmQ5hgj2CVE22wegoe2x652y8HISo-PKgYU2lA5PJK5J4T-Nresrn6dX001iE2ONp_KTFoOuYadaUTPayBDuPmDpAdlqPv4nevS4nI9kZcFHkKDPJgvpy6FvVmUPTW_89IwkthIok3OO0UCO5t52uPW5C8kWgTSgKMZIVY8I=&c=QQhPAuux0tvvITzcB359FvHODaVMKQM6vYfKIQNCu2TQDqgLAebDHQ==&ch=jviaOwMEN6kYdPnCLTzR6LGOUcYAD-u8p5EjunEbhGucMunQvpOUkA==)

name: Seth Koslow for Nassau County Executive
subject: Koslow: Nassau Less Safe Under Blakeman’s Failed Leadership
body:
Violent crime up over 47%, police short-staffed, and community safety programs shut down under current County Executive

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us7.campaign-archive.com/?e=d16fb2f63d&u=85a59214080f8affeb931e7fd&id=0283282ee7)
---
|
---
| | **FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE****Contact: Sean, (540)623-7870****Press@KoslowForNassau.com**
---
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | _**Nassau Less Safe Under Blakeman’s Failed Leadership**_
---
| | _Violent crime up over 47%, police short-staffed, and community safety programs shut down under current County Executive_
---
| | **Nassau County, NY** —Nassau County Legislator, former prosecutor, and County Executive candidate Seth Koslow today slammed Bruce Blakeman for making Nassau County less safe, citing skyrocketing crime rates, police staffing shortages, and cuts to critical community safety programs.
“Bruce Blakeman is a straw man in an empty suit,” said Legislator and County Executive candidate Seth Koslow. “Nowhere is this more evident than in his inability to keep our communities safe. During his first year as County Executive, [violent crime exploded by over 47%](https://koslowfornassau.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=85a59214080f8affeb931e7fd&id=b03ecfd332&e=d16fb2f63d) — and four years later, crime is up across the board [by nearly 30%](https://koslowfornassau.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=85a59214080f8affeb931e7fd&id=1e9dd330ba&e=d16fb2f63d). We have staffing shortages in our law enforcement agencies, we've fallen behind surrounding counties in terms of pay structures, and [Blakeman has even shut down the critical Safe Center ](https://koslowfornassau.us7.list-manage.com/track/click?u=85a59214080f8affeb931e7fd&id=191005da1e&e=d16fb2f63d) that supports domestic violence and sexual assault survivors.”
“Bruce Blakeman doesn’t care about community safety — he cares about publicity stunts and whatever advances his political career. That includes his publicly funded and armed militia of undertrained private citizens. It’s time to call it what it is: Bruce Blakeman is a sham artist who has been robbing the people of Nassau County blind.”
---
| | **A bout Seth Koslow** _Seth Koslow is not a typical politician. He’s a lifelong Nassau County resident, dad, coach, and former prosecutor._
_Born and raised in Baldwin, Seth grew up watching his father achieve the Nassau County Dream, starting a small CPA business from their home and expanding it into a thriving practice that provided Seth and his siblings with the opportunity to attend college and build their own futures._
_Now, Seth is running for Nassau County Executive because taxes are too high, the county wastes too much money, and regular folks are paying the price._

---
|
---
| | | | | | | |
---
| | _Copyright (C) 2025 Koslow for Nassau. All rights reserved._

clean_body: Violent crime up over 47%, police short-staffed, and community safety programs shut down under current County Executive | | | | | | | | | | FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASEContact: Sean, (540)623-7870Press@KoslowForNassau.com | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Nassau Less Safe Under Blakeman’s Failed Leadership | | Violent crime up over 47%, police short-staffed, and community safety programs shut down under current County Executive | | Nassau County, NY —Nassau County Legislator, former prosecutor, and County Executive candidate Seth Koslow today slammed Bruce Blakeman for making Nassau County less safe, citing skyrocketing crime rates, police staffing shortages, and cuts to critical community safety programs. “Bruce Blakeman is a straw man in an empty suit,” said Legislator and County Executive candidate Seth Koslow. “Nowhere is this more evident than in his inability to keep our communities safe. During his first year as County Executive, — and four years later, crime is up across the board . We have staffing shortages in our law enforcement agencies, we've fallen behind surrounding counties in terms of pay structures, and that supports domestic violence and sexual assault survivors.” “Bruce Blakeman doesn’t care about community safety — he cares about publicity stunts and whatever advances his political career. That includes his publicly funded and armed militia of undertrained private citizens. It’s time to call it what it is: Bruce Blakeman is a sham artist who has been robbing the people of Nassau County blind.” | | A bout Seth Koslow Seth Koslow is not a typical politician. He’s a lifelong Nassau County resident, dad, coach, and former prosecutor. Born and raised in Baldwin, Seth grew up watching his father achieve the Nassau County Dream, starting a small CPA business from their home and expanding it into a thriving practice that provided Seth and his siblings with the opportunity to attend college and build their own futures. Now, Seth is running for Nassau County Executive because taxes are too high, the county wastes too much money, and regular folks are paying the price. | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2025 Koslow for Nassau. All rights reserved.

subject: The week Missouri stopped caring

Missouri's latest power grabs are the worst of brazen, cynical politics. Plenty of other states are doing the same thing.

# [The week Missouri stopped caring](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1MDk5NTEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDEwMTk1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vUdQIhjkhX5I88cgULksrBCyM9e3pfwXq99dWraR99c)
### Missouri's latest power grabs are the worst of brazen, cynical politics. Plenty of other states are doing the same thing.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Sep 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NzUwOTk1MSwiZXhwIjoxNzYwMTAxOTUxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.68044i3s_-oChUhFVsPnkc01uFWHu_10EQj08xxiCJ0&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1MDk5NTEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDEwMTk1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vUdQIhjkhX5I88cgULksrBCyM9e3pfwXq99dWraR99c&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1MDk5NTEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDEwMTk1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vUdQIhjkhX5I88cgULksrBCyM9e3pfwXq99dWraR99c)
---
| | [](https://substack..TjMYCcSskQx34IIaXY7ZLjCd_xMQcEKjZnfEd_-LEPM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/bensamuels/p/the-week-missouri-stopped-caring?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

There are two phrases that adorn the [Missouri state flag](https://substack.com/redirect/0bae58a0-7ca3-4964-8f8e-2d0e0e1006bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).1
1. [Salus populi suprema lex esto](https://substack.com/redirect/9e01bbeb-35d3-4a33-b333-d2af7ff8420e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), a Latin phrase that translates roughly to "what's good for the people is the supreme law."2
2. United we stand, divided we fall.

If you've had the misfortune of paying attention to Missouri politics in the last week or so, what you'll notice: our elected officials seem to be doing the exact opposite.
Everything is designed to divide; no consideration is given to what's best for Missourians. They're [gerrymandering our Congressional map](https://substack.com/redirect/151a646c-780f-46fb-bd86-3c1d0a29ff1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in ways that'll harm the whole state; they're making it harder for citizens to [pass ballot measures](https://substack.com/redirect/e125944b-0020-4b66-9e0e-986a07403559?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); they're [fanning the flames of anger and division](https://substack.com/redirect/feab8aec-346c-47c5-9c37-b5adc44929cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Why bother with public welfare and unity when you can steamroll through both on your way to power?
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU3NTA5OTUxLCJleHAiOjIwNzMwODU5NTEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.cJryVDrTilXw0cdtgYonf8S6gxwYynK8NllLE9fzycg?)
> _**Article summary:**_
> * _The state legislature continues to strip Missourians of their voice, and they seem to be getting more and more creative with their tactics to do so._
> * _The issues here go beyond anything partisan. Kansas City is the reason that Missouri is still growing; the latest gerrymander will hurt the region and the state._
> * _On a more uplifting note: hope isn 't lost. There are diverse groups coming together to fight the legislature's brazen power grabs._
>

### Salus factionis suprema lex esto
Forget what's on the flag, that's the new Missouri state motto: what's good for the party is the supreme law.3
The state legislature is now meeting for its [second special session of the year](https://substack.com/redirect/a93a9cdf-dd8f-44bb-9616-7ec059ef751c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). They used the [first special session](https://substack.com/redirect/1f99e54c-2fcc-432e-8711-6fd121daa236?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to take much-needed money from rural hospitals and education so they could siphon it to the owners of the Chiefs.4
[](https://substack.com/redirect/e6888d31-043c-425d-a30f-d563b4ce4219?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Missouri's $1.5 billion stadium grift](https://substack.com/redirect/e6888d31-043c-425d-a30f-d563b4ce4219?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels)| | *
---|---|---
| Jun 2
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/e6888d31-043c-425d-a30f-d563b4ce4219?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
But that's small potatoes relative to what they're doing now.
First, as part of the [national gerrymandering wars](https://substack.com/redirect/ce201c1b-9868-401f-aa39-0beaea9a8010?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [I've written about](https://substack.com/redirect/fc037604-2d92-4d46-9a27-6e3e1e3b4223?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), they're redrawing Congressional maps to [give the Republicans one additional Congressional seat](https://substack.com/redirect/9bb6df1d-ecd8-460a-a557-224693661d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by [chopping up Kansas City](https://substack.com/redirect/620d4515-d4eb-44cd-8405-6fd2364c39fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) into three predominantly rural districts.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/9bb6df1d-ecd8-460a-a557-224693661d72?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As someone who likes to see Democrats win, I've got my objections to this. But even taking off my partisan hat, it's worth noting just how detrimental this Congressional map is for all Missourians.
In these new maps, greater Kansas City becomes a small part of three different districts. What does that mean in practice? Kansas City won't be anyone's primary constituency. Three different members of Congress will represent Kansas City, but for all of them, it'll be just a small chunk of their district.
That'll lead to less focus on and less investment in Kansas City. Needless to say, that's very bad. Missouri _needs_ a thriving Kansas City, whose metro area has driven over half of the state's population growth since 2010.5
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f270fd96-a5f7-4a3a-a5f3-3724c93baca0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**For context: Missouri would be[the sixth slowest-growing state in the U.S. since 2010](https://substack.com/redirect/cf5b1d9c-6e86-443a-8918-3e31670bd45f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) without greater Kansas City**. It's the demographic engine of the state, and what they're doing with this gerrymander makes it difficult for anyone to properly represent Kansas City in Congress.6
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1MDk5NTEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDEwMTk1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vUdQIhjkhX5I88cgULksrBCyM9e3pfwXq99dWraR99c)
"United we stand, divided we fall" is right there on our flag. This is a much more literal division than the original flagmakers could've ever imagined. And we'll all suffer for it.7
But the second thing that the legislature is doing is much more insidious.
### Government is bad...unless we're the ones controlling it
Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives Mike Johnson, on his official website, outlines "[7 Core Principles of Conservatism](https://substack.com/redirect/8c6b7b94-8f52-490f-885a-dd22bc4f5652?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." The very first one:
> The birth of our great nation was inspired by the bold declaration that our individual, God-given liberties should be preserved against government intrusion. That same conviction informs our conservative policy decisions still today.
Going back to the ['50s](https://substack.com/redirect/06ba7d4f-d74c-43bc-aec6-52e5f74e6a0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and ['60s](https://substack.com/redirect/383ea438-91dc-4af7-baaa-bdf45be1e374?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), through the Reagan years and into the present, this idea that individuals are better at making decisions for themselves than the government has been a bedrock of the conservative movement.
That guiding principle is part of the reason citizen-initiated ballot measures exist in the first place.8
It's a critical check on the power of state legislatures, and over the past decade, it's allowed Missourians to pass everything from [nonpartisan redistricting](https://substack.com/redirect/afedaa49-b82f-43a4-911a-87a16e9a1822?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [abortion access](https://substack.com/redirect/8a678b4d-5f36-4b96-bf5d-06a6b449ad00?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to a [higher minimum wage](https://substack.com/redirect/11f3a6d0-469d-4c40-bb2c-28f63a135bdb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).9
Missouri's legislature has done a great job rolling back voter-approved policies through different machinations--a misleading ballot measure to [get rid of nonpartisan redistricting](https://substack.com/redirect/0651378a-5d9a-445c-9192-a96e4b16ac88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [putting up hurdles around abortion access](https://substack.com/redirect/70ceb68d-8b5d-4932-af04-183cd7ecc881?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and a [straight-up repeal of the new minimum wage law](https://substack.com/redirect/8a45df9e-da17-4bc4-9fa8-22f220bd4f37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) just months after voters approved it.
But for Missouri's legislature, rolling back the will of the voters piecemeal isn't enough.10 Now, the legislature is trying to functionally take away this right altogether by [requiring a majority of voters ](https://substack.com/redirect/4af8d985-f454-4965-a2d5-433644b9bc0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[and](https://substack.com/redirect/4af8d985-f454-4965-a2d5-433644b9bc0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ a majority of Congressional districts to pass constitutional amendments](https://substack.com/redirect/4af8d985-f454-4965-a2d5-433644b9bc0b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Combined with gerrymandering, what this means: "[As few as 5% of voters could defeat initiative petitions.](https://substack.com/redirect/f6b83f65-12e7-4399-8522-b5302eef1dbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" Which is, of course, the intent.
Conservatives love the idea of checks on government power in theory. Just never in practice.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=173104741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTc1MDk5NTEsImV4cCI6MTc2MDEwMTk1MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vUdQIhjkhX5I88cgULksrBCyM9e3pfwXq99dWraR99c)
### I wish that were everything
Thought that was the extent of divisive elected officials doing things to make our lives harder? Nope. Also from the past week:
* There's an [enormous backlog of public records requests](https://substack.com/redirect/c8d9173d-8f3e-475e-8661-5b0b60f2dc08?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which are critical to knowing what our government is doing and saying behind the scenes, and a critical part of how reporters do their jobs. We have former Attorney General Andrew Bailey to thank for that, who [slow-rolled responses](https://substack.com/redirect/d88cf9c7-18ae-47ca-9542-68c82325836d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* Republican Missouri Senator Eric Schmitt delivered [an anti-immigrant screed](https://substack.com/redirect/feab8aec-346c-47c5-9c37-b5adc44929cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at the National Conservatism Conference, despite the fact that a) Missouri has [one of the lowest foreign-born populations of any state](https://substack.com/redirect/be9515e0-89fb-43bc-b3b9-4669e8860d2a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the country, and b) [we have 150,000+ unfilled jobs](https://substack.com/redirect/06311c59-3e0c-4a38-8469-b8e841b22b09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and an above-average unfilled job rate. Immigrants could help fill that gap.

### Where there's hope
We've got our work cut out for us. But it's not hopeless.
First, **I 'm not convinced that the legislature will succeed in stripping away our power to introduce ballot measures**, because voters will still have to approve the measure. [North Dakota](https://substack.com/redirect/70d23592-c083-4352-8a6a-8d36ad240007?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Ohio](https://substack.com/redirect/36ebd876-d8f9-4779-b5f0-2043e2323a96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) both tried this gambit and both failed; voters (rightly) don't like reducing their own voting power!
Politics makes strange bedfellows, and people are lining up to defeat the measure: everyone from the [AFL-CIO](https://substack.com/redirect/a4e8987b-924b-4d04-ba03-8437d8f2c7dd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the [Missouri Association of Realtors](https://substack.com/redirect/74fa529e-fe4f-4041-85c6-f0cbde240271?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
As for gerrymandering, there are early [efforts underway to repeal the gerrymander](https://substack.com/redirect/36839b0e-5222-4555-9ff3-41d552a8d7e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) via the ballot--something [I mentioned two weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/ee852b00-c4bd-4992-8406-a3c0f704b56c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as a possibility. And it's a winnable fight: [gerrymandering is unpopular](https://substack.com/redirect/10340469-ab73-40a3-ab7d-14f5375d6edd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [even in Republican states like Missouri](https://substack.com/redirect/0a0eccb4-c661-4804-b64c-5da81586685d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The fight isn't over yet. It's just beginning. But we'll be fighting on their terms, and that puts sanity--and what's good for Missourians--at a disadvantage from the get-go.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTczMTA0NzQxLCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzU3NTA5OTUxLCJleHAiOjIwNzMwODU5NTEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.cJryVDrTilXw0cdtgYonf8S6gxwYynK8NllLE9fzycg?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/1c2e2c65-4a95-483f-9112-8be58d47bc6f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
Missouri's flag is pretty underwhelming, but [evidently it leads the nation](https://substack.com/redirect/05cba45a-9938-4bff-a6b6-49693cdb3b3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in both "number of bears" and "number of stars." So that's fun! You'll enjoy the video if you're into [vexillology](https://substack.com/redirect/5f8aea7f-93fd-4ebc-bee5-7b62ba8970d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
2
An expression I'm only familiar with, somewhat embarrassingly, because it's what Kenneth says to Jack Donaghy [after rejecting a bed-bug-infested hug in an episode of ](https://substack.com/redirect/9b43ca00-c56d-4448-a3c2-667a7bafe402?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[30 Rock](https://substack.com/redirect/9b43ca00-c56d-4448-a3c2-667a7bafe402?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_. (Sorry, I couldn't find the actual clip on YouTube, so you'll have to watch the full episode to get there.)
In any event, there are different ways to translate this phrase--[Wikipedia references a few different options](https://substack.com/redirect/9e01bbeb-35d3-4a33-b333-d2af7ff8420e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--so I'm sticking to one that more closely aligns with [the state's official translation](https://substack.com/redirect/1e428404-f9fe-4f4d-80e0-c45b273aeab6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
3
Salus populi suprema lex esto--translated by Missouri as "let the good of the people be the supreme law"--is [Missouri's official state motto](https://substack.com/redirect/1e428404-f9fe-4f4d-80e0-c45b273aeab6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). I assumed it would have something to do with the "Show-Me State" nickname, but no, it's been this Latin phrase for more than 200 years.
4
[Whose net worth](https://substack.com/redirect/e622a5cd-4b67-415f-ab52-67c89888fc69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I will once again note, is $25 billion. They do not need taxpayer subsidy.
5
Data here comes from the list of counties that make up [Greater Kansas City](https://substack.com/redirect/f8903eb9-dbfe-4ab1-8f3b-35539e209530?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (Bates, Caldwell, Cass, Clay, Clinton, Jackson, Lafayette, Platte, and Ray Counties), [Census data](https://substack.com/redirect/72364e15-d9bc-44f6-87a4-d5a7daf71d88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and of course, [Wikipedia](https://substack.com/redirect/8de57ff9-6c5b-4db5-9811-289c0be3d040?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Greater Kansas City includes lots of Kansas (including, but not limited to, Kansas City, Kansas). Any data from Kansas has been excluded to make it an apples-to-apples Missouri comparison.
Finally, I'm resharing this post I wrote a few months ago, which is relevant and (in my opinion) one of the best analyses I've written:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/a4f64c55-247e-4cab-9242-57f5507c170b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Missouri's insanely bloated government(s)](https://substack.com/redirect/a4f64c55-247e-4cab-9242-57f5507c170b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels)| | *
---|---|---
| Apr 8
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/a4f64c55-247e-4cab-9242-57f5507c170b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
6
That goes beyond just policymaking: corralling resources, advocating for infrastructure funding, running constituent services, etc.
7
Even [Governor Mike Kehoe isn't that into it](https://substack.com/redirect/ab88cf2f-e613-4d25-b8cc-effcad38b1da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), but national pressure is just too great, and like just about every Republican in this era, he caved.
8
At the state level, the right for citizens to circumvent their state legislatures this way emerged in places across the U.S. during the so-called [Progressive Era](https://substack.com/redirect/73ba7c66-1548-4475-b343-f0e93e2fd396?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), when voters were pushing back against monopolies, income inequality, poverty, and entrenched political interests with too much power.
9
In fairness, it was also used to pass sports gambling, which becomes legal on [December 1](https://substack.com/redirect/bfcfd458-083c-44ac-ab5c-816b9892417b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). If you've been watching sports in Missouri lately, just about every commercial is about how soon you'll be able to start gambling.
Anyway, in case you need the reminder, this is bad and we shouldn't quit fighting to outlaw it in Missouri:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/89d67457-c078-46bf-8879-f26622b483bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [Online gambling is dangerous. Missourians should vote against it.](https://substack.com/redirect/89d67457-c078-46bf-8879-f26622b483bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels)| | *
---|---|---
| September 23, 2024
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/89d67457-c078-46bf-8879-f26622b483bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
10
Nor is [sending voters flagrantly misleading language](https://substack.com/redirect/72ca3c79-7431-4ef3-a116-db710266463d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). For context, this is the proposed ballot language for the _repeal_ of abortion that we'll vote on in '26.
> * Guarantee access to care for medical emergencies, ectopic pregnancies, and miscarriages;
> * Ensure women's safety during abortions;
> * Ensure parental consent for minors;
> * Allow abortions for medical emergencies, fetal anomalies, rape, and incest;
> * Require physicians to provide medically accurate information; and
> * Protect children from gender transition?
>

This is, to state the obvious, insane, and designed to be actively confusing. It's an abortion ban couched as an abortion guarantee! They're doing this because they know that they can't win the issue on its merits.
I do think it'll ultimately get rewritten in the courts, but we'll see.

Plenty of other states are doing the same thing. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more Missouri's latest power grabs are the worst of brazen, cynical politics. Plenty of other states are doing the same thing. | | | Sep 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | There are two phrases that adorn the 1. , a Latin phrase that translates roughly to "what's good for the people is the supreme law."2 2. United we stand, divided we fall. If you've had the misfortune of paying attention to Missouri politics in the last week or so, what you'll notice: our elected officials seem to be doing the exact opposite. Everything is designed to divide; no consideration is given to what's best for Missourians. They're in ways that'll harm the whole state; they're making it harder for citizens to ; they're . Why bother with public welfare and unity when you can steamroll through both on your way to power? ? Article summary: The state legislature continues to strip Missourians of their voice, and they seem to be getting more and more creative with their tactics to do so. The issues here go beyond anything partisan. Kansas City is the reason that Missouri is still growing; the latest gerrymander will hurt the region and the state. On a more uplifting note: hope isn 't lost. There are diverse groups coming together to fight the legislature's brazen power grabs. Salus factionis suprema lex esto Forget what's on the flag, that's the new Missouri state motto: what's good for the party is the supreme law.3 The state legislature is now meeting for its . They used the to take much-needed money from rural hospitals and education so they could siphon it to the owners of the Chiefs.4 | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | Jun 2 | But that's small potatoes relative to what they're doing now. First, as part of the , they're redrawing Congressional maps to by into three predominantly rural districts. | | ---|---|--- As someone who likes to see Democrats win, I've got my objections to this. But even taking off my partisan hat, it's worth noting just how detrimental this Congressional map is for all Missourians. In these new maps, greater Kansas City becomes a small part of three different districts. What does that mean in practice? Kansas City won't be anyone's primary constituency. Three different members of Congress will represent Kansas City, but for all of them, it'll be just a small chunk of their district. That'll lead to less focus on and less investment in Kansas City. Needless to say, that's very bad. Missouri needs a thriving Kansas City, whose metro area has driven over half of the state's population growth since 2010.5 | | ---|---|--- For context: Missouri would be without greater Kansas City. It's the demographic engine of the state, and what they're doing with this gerrymander makes it difficult for anyone to properly represent Kansas City in Congress.6 "United we stand, divided we fall" is right there on our flag. This is a much more literal division than the original flagmakers could've ever imagined. And we'll all suffer for it.7 But the second thing that the legislature is doing is much more insidious. Government is bad...unless we're the ones controlling it Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives Mike Johnson, on his official website, outlines "." The very first one: The birth of our great nation was inspired by the bold declaration that our individual, God-given liberties should be preserved against government intrusion. That same conviction informs our conservative policy decisions still today. Going back to the and , through the Reagan years and into the present, this idea that individuals are better at making decisions for themselves than the government has been a bedrock of the conservative movement. That guiding principle is part of the reason citizen-initiated ballot measures exist in the first place.8 It's a critical check on the power of state legislatures, and over the past decade, it's allowed Missourians to pass everything from to to a Missouri's legislature has done a great job rolling back voter-approved policies through different machinations--a misleading ballot measure to , , and a just months after voters approved it. But for Missouri's legislature, rolling back the will of the voters piecemeal isn't enough.10 Now, the legislature is trying to functionally take away this right altogether by . Combined with gerrymandering, what this means: "" Which is, of course, the intent. Conservatives love the idea of checks on government power in theory. Just never in practice. I wish that were everything Thought that was the extent of divisive elected officials doing things to make our lives harder? Nope. Also from the past week: There's an , which are critical to knowing what our government is doing and saying behind the scenes, and a critical part of how reporters do their jobs. We have former Attorney General Andrew Bailey to thank for that, who . Republican Missouri Senator Eric Schmitt delivered at the National Conservatism Conference, despite the fact that a) Missouri has in the country, and b) and an above-average unfilled job rate. Immigrants could help fill that gap. Where there's hope We've got our work cut out for us. But it's not hopeless. First, I 'm not convinced that the legislature will succeed in stripping away our power to introduce ballot measures, because voters will still have to approve the measure. and both tried this gambit and both failed; voters (rightly) don't like reducing their own voting power! Politics makes strange bedfellows, and people are lining up to defeat the measure: everyone from the to the . As for gerrymandering, there are early via the ballot--something as a possibility. And it's a winnable fight: , . The fight isn't over yet. It's just beginning. But we'll be fighting on their terms, and that puts sanity--and what's good for Missourians--at a disadvantage from the get-go. ? It reminded me that I can't give up on life, give up on my family, or give up on my country. ______________________________________ President Trump OFFICIALLY PARDONED ME on January 20th, 2025. It was an honor to meet him and say thank you for giving me and 1,500 J6 prisoners our freedom back. ______________________________________ I will never take my freedom for granted again. That 's why I'm running for U.S. Congress - something I NEVER imagined doing. Where I'm from in West Virginia, we don't trust politicians. And for good reason. The D.C. elites have sold us out --shipping our coal, lumber, and factory jobs to China. Allowing millions of illegal aliens to take over our neighborhoods and undercut our wages. Wasting our tax dollars overseas while rural America crumbles. West Virginians aren't the only ones feeling this. Hard-working families are getting CRUSHED in all 50 states. I'm fighting to fix that. This is the most important race of my life -- and it won't be easy. I'm not backed by billionaires or political "experts". My campaign is 100% powered by patriots like you. If you're tired of all of the lies, corruption, and tone-deaf leadership in Washington --I need you in this fight with me. 99% of Republicans will ignore this message. Even fewer will donate. Will you be the 1% who steps up in a major way? Let 's take our country back and finish what we started under President Trump. THANK YOU FOR YOUR SUPPORT! Derrick Evans Former Biden Regime Prisoner Republican for U.S. Congress | | | | Paid for by Evans for WV 50 MOUNTAINEER DR PRICHARD, WV 25555 | | Pop Acta Media | Your America PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

name: TN House Democratic Caucus
subject: A Death Warrant for Rural Tennessee Families
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
A $1 Trillion Cut to Medicaid? We can’t let this happen.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4STKWRGKM1MFNE2G5ZCXENM_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4STKWRGKM1MFNE2G5ZCXENM_1)
---
| | | | Peter, The One Big Beautiful Bill Act passed by Republicans in Washington is actually One Big Steaming Pile of Sh*t. The harmful law is being called a “death warrant” by families across the country and for good reason. It **slashes nearly $1 TRILLION in Medicaid funding** over the next decade. Millions, including children with life-threatening conditions, will be forced to navigate a labyrinth of red tape and endless paperwork just to stay alive or lose coverage. In rural Tennessee communities, one parent is quoted as saying _“If we make one wrong decision as the parents of a critically ill child, that could be the end of it,”_ referring to the mountains of paperwork. Here in Tennessee, we’ve seen these attacks before. **The House Democratic Caucus has consistently sought to expand TennCare and fought back against TennCare cuts and other GOP attempts to dismantle public healthcare.** While Republicans work to strip healthcare away from our most vulnerable neighbors, Democrats are fighting to protect them. **This is not a partisan fight.** This is a fight for our children and most vulnerable Tennesseans. Help power our campaign to protect healthcare in Tennessee. Help us block this latest, heartless attack on healthcare.
---
| | [ Donate Now ](https://ctrk.klclick1.com/l/01K4STKWRGKM1MFNE2G5ZCXENM_2)
---
|
---
| — Tennessee House Democratic Caucus
---

---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_

body:

| | **THIS IS AN AUTOMATED MESSAGE FOR TOP DEMOCRATS**

We’re reaching to Democrats who have not added their response to our time-sensitive poll about Trump’s plan to BAN mail-in voting.

**This could completely derail our chances for 2026!_While Trump tries to RIG the midterms, we need to know where you stand:_**

[**Please confirm your signature is submitted before the deadline: DO YOU SUPPORT VOTE-BY-MAIL? »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/poll-vote-by-mail/?source=VPP_20250910_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS_RS&t=6&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6) | [YES, WE MUST PROTECT VOTING RIGHTS! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/poll-vote-by-mail/?source=VPP_20250910_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS_RS&t=7&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6)

**⬇️ READ OUR ORIGINAL MESSAGE BELOW ⬇️**
**PLEASE DON’T LOOK AWAY:**

→ Donald Trump wants REVENGE on mail-in voting after it handed him a HUGE DEFEAT in 2020.

→ His executive order is one of the worst attacks on voting rights in HISTORY.

→ **If we don’t show MASSIVE support for vote-by-mail, MILLIONS of Americans risk having their ballots _thrown in the TRASH!!!_**
[**So we need to hear from YOU:
DO YOU SUPPORT VOTE-BY-MAIL?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/poll-vote-by-mail/?source=VPP_20250910_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS_RS&t=10&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6) | [YES, WE MUST PROTECT VOTING RIGHTS! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/poll-vote-by-mail/?source=VPP_20250910_EMPOLL_E1_T0_NS_RS&t=11&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6)

If we don’t receive 5,000 responses by midnight, our momentum to save vote-by-mail will be lost. **Please respond ASAP!**

_The Voter Protection Project_
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6).**

Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=13&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=21177%2E878179%2EfAQaR6)
